metaTitle: Thermostatic Radiator Valve | Fault Signs and Replacement
metaDescription: Learn what a thermostatic radiator valve does, signs it has failed and when it may need replacing.
intro: A thermostatic radiator valve helps control how much hot water flows through an individual radiator. When it sticks or fails, one radiator may stay cold or behave unpredictably.
replacementCost: Replacement cost usually depends on valve type, access and whether the wider system also has circulation or pressure issues.
safetyWarning: A stuck radiator valve may be one part of a bigger system issue, so diagnosis still matters.
symptoms: One radiator stays cold while others work
The valve head does not seem to affect radiator output

sections.heading: What the valve does
sections.body: A thermostatic radiator valve adjusts water flow through a single radiator based on the local temperature setting.

sections.heading: How failure usually appears
sections.body: If the valve sticks shut or stops responding, the radiator may remain cold even though the rest of the heating system is running.

sections.heading: When replacement makes sense
sections.body: If the valve is confirmed faulty, replacement is usually more sensible than repeated attempts to work around the issue.
